methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
RMFG is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Kenneth Cassel that has raised $5M+. The valuation universe comprises 9 private peers and 1 public comparable in the Robotics & Manufacturing sector, including companies such as 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

The Stage-Progression method was the applicable triangulation approach. Using total capital raised of $5M as a proxy for Seed-stage value and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier for progression to the next funding round, this method implies a valuation of $12M. The Peer-Median approach was not applicable; while 11 same-sector peers exist, fewer than three qualified as same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with similar valuation tiers, rendering a narrow peer analysis unreliable.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $11M to $14M, with a median point of $12M. This range is narrow and reflects reliance on a single method rather than convergent triangulation. Key uncertainties include the absence of sufficient stage-matched private peers and the limited public comp set, which constrain calibration of stage-progression multipliers to this specific company's profile. Additional diligence on runway, burn rate, and product development milestones would strengthen conviction in this range.
